series.title: [PATCH v1] tests/qtest/migration: Fix slow test dirty_limit

review.summary: The change correctly updates `test_dirty_limit()` to use deferred incoming setup and explicitly issue `migrate-incoming`, matching the post-4e8c4dda97 failure-handling model for destination-side migration tests. I found no functional regressions in the patch itself.

stages.A: Patch intent is clear and consistent with the cited Fixes commit: tests expecting destination failure must no longer rely on automatic incoming-side exit.
stages.B: Code-path review of the modified test logic looks correct: `migrate_start(..., "defer", ...)` is paired with an explicit `migrate_incoming_qmp()` before `migrate_qmp()`.
stages.C: Behavioral assessment: this should restore the intended ordering for a test that expects the destination side to fail, avoiding the previous stale/incorrect startup mode.
stages.D: API/helper usage appears internally consistent. `args->connect_uri` remains the URI used for both the deferred incoming activation and the source-side migrate command.
stages.E: No checkpatch, build, or obvious maintainability issues are introduced by the delta. Residual risk is low and limited to runtime semantics already described by the commit message.

disclaimer: LLM-generated draft. Not an authoritative review.
